channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Here's what happened during President Donald Trump's eighth week in office
item.description: President Donald Trump blasted former President Joe Biden’s Department of Justice, accusing the agency of becoming the “department of injustice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal agencies plan for mass layoffs as Trump's workforce cuts continue
item.description: Thursday's deadline for federal agencies to submit plans for large-scale layoffs kicks off a new phase in the dramatic restructuring of how the government operates. Here's what that looks like.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Democrats confront limits of their minority power after bruising shutdown vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democrats were grim Friday as they left Washington after a brutal 10-week stretch that consistently showed the limits of their power in the minority — and culminated with a deeply personal rupture over how to best counter President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he was being a 'bit sarcastic' when he promised to end Russia-Ukraine war in 24 hours
item.description: President Donald Trump says he was “being a little bit sarcastic” when he repeatedly claimed as a candidate that he would have the Russia-Ukraine war solved within 24 hours — and before he even took office.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Emerging from 2024 loss, Tim Walz holds a town hall in an Iowa Republican's district
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz stood before hundreds of Iowans on Friday and admitted he doesn't have all the answers about issues facing the country. “If I did, we wouldn't be in this goddamn mess,” Walz said.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday that South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the country, in the latest Trump administration move targeting the African nation.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ron Nessen, press secretary to President Ford, dies at 90
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ron Nessen, a veteran broadcast journalist who was press secretary for President Gerald Ford and sought to restore the integrity that the position had lost during the Nixon administration, has died. He was 90.
item.dc:creator: DOUGLASS K. DANIEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Veterans rally in front of Minnesota Republican Party office in Edina
item.description: A group of veterans and supporters say they are against recent funding cuts and layoffs made by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Chandra Colvin, MPR News

item.title: Spending bill to avert shutdown successfully beats filibuster with hours until deadline
item.description: The Senate voted on a key procedural motion regarding the House-passed stopgap spending bill on Friday. If a measure is not passed by midnight, the government will enter a shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As 'match day' nears, med schools, lawmakers want to expand residencies to keep doctors in Nevada
item.description: On Friday, March 21 — just a week away — more than 40,000 medical students around the country will find out where they will be serving their residencies. About 300 of those graduates are from UNLV, UNR and Touro University, but there aren't enough residencies in the state to keep them here.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mace sued for defamation over allegations in stunning floor speech
item.description: A man Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of being part of a group of “predators” in a stunning House floor speech is suing her for defamation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats set to host town halls targeting vulnerable House Republicans
item.description: Democratic committees are set to host town halls across the country to target vulnerable House Republicans ahead of the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Biden White House turned over Trump, Pence government cellphones to FBI as part of anti-Trump agent's case
item.description: The Biden White House turned over government cellphones belonging to President Donald Trump and former Vice President Mike Pence to the FBI in May 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS agents search Columbia dorm rooms after Khalil arrest
item.description: Department of Homeland Security agents searched two dorm rooms at Columbia University Thursday night, the school said, a move that comes less than a week after the federal arrest of former grad student Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund helps former service members sickened by military toxic exposures, including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer says he will vote to advance GOP spending bill, lowering threat of shutdown
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) announced on the Senate floor Thursday that he plans to vote to advance a House-passed six-month government funding bill, despite the strong opposition of many Democrats who say the bill would create a “slush fund” for President Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Oversight Democrats launch review of crypto reserve 'poised to enrich' Trump
item.description: House Oversight Democrats are encouraging President Trump to abandon his plans for a crypto reserve, calling it a strategy to enrich himself at taxpayer expense.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House bill to encourage sharing of immigration data with ICE introduced
item.description: The bill aims to prevent state and local governments from limiting their employees’ ability to share immigration data, including the legal status of immigrants, with federal authorities.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Trump threatens retaliatory 200% tariff on European wine after EU proposes American whiskey tax
item.description: The European Commission is calling on President Donald Trump to revoke tariffs now that he's threatened a 200 percent tariff on European wine, Champagne and spirits if Europe goes forward with a planned tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump vows to take back 'stolen' wealth as tariffs on steel and aluminum imports go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump is openly challenging U.S. allies by increasing tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25 percent as he vows to take back wealth he says was “stolen” by other countries, drawing quick retaliation from Europe and Canada.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: U.S. inflation cooled last month, though trade war threatens to lift prices
item.description: U.S. inflation slowed last month for the first time since September even as additional tariffs on steel and aluminum kicked in Wednesday that could send prices higher.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: A Republican-backed bill would upend voter registration. Here are 8 things to know
item.description: Congressional Republicans are pushing legislation that would make sweeping changes to voter registration, including requiring those signing up to present documents proving U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DUI punishments higher under 2 bills introduced Monday in Nevada Senate
item.description: Republicans are proposing more severe punishment for driving under the influence with two new bills introduced Monday in the Nevada Senate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada sex trafficking law 'nearly a parking ticket' for johns; higher fines, more exposure proposed
item.description: Getting tough on the customers who pay for sex is the best way to attack human trafficking, according to a woman who told her story to lawmakers on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
